title: 10 best herbal essential oils for neck pain
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for neck pain are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate discomfort and tension in the neck and shoulder region. They work by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and improving circulation. The best herbal essential oils for neck pain include Eucalyptus globulus, Zingiber officinale, Cymbopogon citratus, Lavandula angustifolia, Mentha x piperita, Cinnamomum verum, Rosmarinus officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Salvia officinalis, and Cinnamomum zeylanicum. If left untreated, neck pain can lead to migraines, decreased productivity, and strained relationships, significantly impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hazel essential oils helps with neck pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as betulinic acid and hazelnut fatty acids, work together to reduce inflammation and ease tension in the neck muscles. Additionally, hazel essential oil's warming and soothing qualities help to relax the muscles, increasing blood flow and reducing stiffness. This natural combination makes it an effective and popular choice for relieving neck pain and promoting overall relaxation and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal hazel essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the neck muscles, joints, and surrounding tissues.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic and numbing properties of herbal hazel essential oil can help alleviate acute or chronic pain associated with neck strain, sprains, or injuries.

Antispasmodic: Herbal hazel essential oil's antispasmodic properties can help relax tense muscles and reduce spasms in the neck and surrounding areas, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hazel roots contain bioactive compounds like salicin and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving neck pain.
Barks: The bark of the hazel plant contains a high concentration of flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh corylus avellana leaves in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator set at 35 degrees celsius.
Steam distill the dried corylus avellana leaves using a copper still.
Combine the distilled essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba.
Store the prepared herbal essential oil blend in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with neck pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains a compound called citral, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscles. When applied topically to the affected area, lemon balm essential oil can help to ease tension and stiffness in the neck and shoulders, providing relief from chronic pain and discomfort. Its calming effects can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, further contributing to its effectiveness in alleviating neck pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Herbal lemon balm essential oil's antispasmodic property helps to relax tense muscles and relieve spasms in the neck, reducing pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm essential oil's anti-inflammatory property reduces swelling and inflammation in the affected area, which can help alleviate neck pain caused by injuries or conditions like arthritis.

Sedative property: The sedative properties of herbal lemon balm essential oil help to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and tension that can exacerbate neck pain and promoting relaxation and sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have been traditionally used to treat muscle and joint pain, making them a good source for essential oils that can help alleviate neck pain.

Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain high concentrations of citral and geraniol, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to relax muscles, making them effective in treating neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn or late afternoon when their aroma is most potent and store them in an airtight container.
Chop 20g of the harvested leaves into small pieces and combine them with 200ml of high-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 21 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate the extraction process and monitor its progress visually.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and reserving the resulting essential oil-rich extract.
Dilute the extracted essential oil with a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil at a 1:10 ratio and store it in dark glass bottles for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with neck pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area. The oil's soothing and calming effects also help to ease muscle tension and stiffness, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced pain. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant properties help to promote relaxation and calmness, reducing stress and promoting a sense of well-being that can contribute to alleviating neck pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the neck, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating tension and stiffness.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of chamomile essential oil help to relax muscles and ease spasms, reducing muscle cramps and tightness that can contribute to neck pain.
Sedative: Chamomile essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce anxiety and stress that can exacerbate neck pain, promoting relaxation and restful sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce swelling and pain in the muscles and joints.
Flowers: The flowers of chamomile contain alpha-bisabolol, a soothing agent that helps to relax muscles and reduce inflammation in the neck and shoulder region.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers from a well-known organic source.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, dark place for at least 14 days.
Steam distill the dried flowers using 10% of flower material to carrier oil ratio and 2 hours of distillation time.
Collect the obtained essential oil and filter it through a paper coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep at room temperature.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of herbal broad-leaved lime essential oil can help relax tense muscles and reduce spasms in the neck, promoting relaxation and reducing stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for relieving neck pain.
Barks: Containing terpenes and phenolic acids, barks have been traditionally used to treat musculoskeletal issues, including neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos flowers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency retention.
Dry the flowers using air circulation at room temperature within 24 hours of harvesting for maximum quality preservation.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a 20% flower-to-water ratio with 30 minutes of distillation time for efficient oil extraction.
Combine the extracted essential oil with 10% jojoba oil as a carrier agent for smooth and gentle application on neck areas.
Bottle and store the finished tilia platyphyllos essential oil in dark glass at room temperature for up to 6 months with proper label disclosure.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with neck pain because it contains compounds that possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When applied topically, these compounds work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the muscles and joints of the neck, providing relief from tension headaches and stiffness. Additionally, the warm, comforting aroma of Ceylon cinnamon can help promote relaxation, reducing stress and promoting a sense of calm that further alleviates neck pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, the underground stem of the plant, contains compounds that help relieve muscle spasms and stiffness in the neck, making it a popular choice for aromatherapy.
Barks: The barks of the Ceylon cinnamon tree are rich in cinnamaldehyde, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the neck area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum leaves in the morning when dew is still present for optimal aroma and potency.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the washed leaves in a warm, dry place for 7-10 days or until they reach a moisture level of 12%.
Steam distill the dried cinnamomum verum leaves using 20 kg of fresh leaves and 100 liters of distilled water for 2 hours.
Separate the resulting essential oil from the distilled water using a separation funnel and collect the extracted oil in a sterile glass bottle.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to neck pain caused by muscle strain or injury.
Analgesic properties: The analgesic compounds present in Ceylon cinnamon essential oil can help numb the pain receptors in the neck area, providing relief from acute or chronic pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the neck muscles and joints, which can contribute to pain and stiffness.

Antispasmodic: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has been found to relax muscle spasms and reduce contractions in the neck region, which can help alleviate tension headaches, migraines, and other conditions that contribute to neck pain.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from the plant, ensuring they are free of pests and diseases, approximately 10:00 am to ensure optimal oil yield.
Clean and sort the harvested leaves by removing any stems or damaged parts, using a fine-mesh sieve over a bowl to separate debris, taking about 15 minutes.
Steam distill the clean leaves in a copper still, with water at a ratio of 1:1, maintaining a temperature range of 100-160°c for 2 hours, to release essential oils and aroma compounds.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the condenser coil, filtering it through a paper filter or coffee filter into dark glass bottles, taking about 10 minutes.

Store the final eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a cool, dark place for at least 24 hours before use to allow any impurities to settle and clarity to develop, ensuring optimal potency and shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with neck pain because it has a cooling and refreshing effect that can ease tension and stiffness in the muscles. When applied topically to the affected area, peppermint oil's menthol content helps to reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms, providing relief from chronic pain and discomfort. Its calming properties also help to soothe and calm the mind, reducing stress and promoting a sense of relaxation, which can contribute to overall neck pain relief.
remedy_properties: Pain Relieving Properties: Peppermint essential oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce pain and stiffness in the neck, providing relief from discomfort and tension.
Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The menthol content in peppermint oil helps to reduce inflammation in the muscles and joints of the neck, which can contribute to pain and limited mobility.
Muscle Relaxant Properties: Peppermint essential oil's ability to relax muscle spasms and cramps in the neck helps to alleviate tension and strain, promoting a sense of relaxation and reduced pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make essential oils that help to relax muscles and reduce tension in the neck, promoting a sense of calmness.
Leaves: Fresh or dried peppermint leaves are used to create essential oils that help to ease muscle spasms and stiffness in the neck, promoting relaxation and reducing pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or store-bought ones, allowing 50g of leaves for every 10ml of oil needed.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a well-ventilated area away from direct sunlight, taking about 3 days to achieve optimal dryness.
Chop the dried peppermint leaves into small pieces using scissors or mortar and pestle, ensuring uniformity for efficient steam distillation.
Load the chopped peppermint leaves into your steam distillation apparatus, adding 100ml of water and allowing the process to run for 2 hours at a temperature range of 60-80°c.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil, discarding any solids or impurities, and storing the final product in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with neck pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia officinalis leaves measuring approximately 100g fresh or 50g dried.
Steam-distill the harvested leaves in a copper alembic still using 500ml of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the distillate using a separator funnel at 45°c.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids protected from light and heat.
Mix 2 drops of the salvia officinalis essential oil with 10ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with neck pain because of its calming and relaxing properties, which can help to reduce muscle tension and inflammation in the neck. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers in the early morning when dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels.
Steam distill the dried lavender flowers using 10% plant material and 90% water at atmospheric pressure.
Collect the distilled essential oil after 45 minutes to an hour of processing time.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal oil quality and potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat setting for 6 hours or air dry for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 20 grams of material per 1 liter of water at atmospheric pressure.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat neck pain with herbal essential oils is to blend Frankincense, Wintergreen, and Helichrysum oils. Apply a few drops of the mixture to the affected area using gentle massage strokes or inhale it through steam inhalation. The anti-inflammatory properties of Frankincense and Wintergreen help reduce pain and swelling, while Helichrysum promotes healing and relaxation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to neck pain that are treated with herbal essential oils are headaches, migraines, and shoulder stiffness. For instance, peppermint oil can help relieve tension headaches by cooling the skin and soothing muscle contractions. Similarly, lavender oil can ease migraines by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Meanwhile, eucalyptus oil can loosen tight shoulders and improve circulation to alleviate stiffness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eucalyptol, interact with the body's natural pathways to reduce inflammation and ease pain. When applied topically to the affected area, Eucalyptus globulus oil can increase blood flow and relax tense muscles, providing quick relief from neck strain and discomfort. remedy_constituents: Cineole: It helps reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from neck pain.
Eucalyptol: It acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic, helping to ease pain and discomfort in the neck.
α-Pinene: It has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax muscles and reduce stiffness in the neck.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of Eucalyptus essential oil due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, which provides pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Eucalyptus buds are used to create a more concentrated and potent essential oil, often used for its decongestant and analgesic properties to alleviate neck pain.
Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus are also used to produce essential oil, which contains eucalyptol and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves with sharp pruning shears in the early morning.
Steam distill the harvested leaves in a 4-liter distillation apparatus for 4-6 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a 100ml glass container with a capacity of 10-20%.
Filter the collected oil through a 0.2-micron membrane filter to remove impurities immediately.
Store the filtered oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties and warming effects. The oil, commonly known as ginger essential oil, contains compounds that reduce muscle spasms and relax tense muscles, relieving tension in the neck and shoulders. Additionally, its analgesic and antispasmodic properties ease pain and discomfort associated with neck strain. remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Helps to reduce pain and inflammation by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
Shogaol: Reduces muscle spasms and pain by blocking the activity of pain receptors in the body.
Zingiberene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and pain in the neck and surrounding areas.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate neck pain.
Roots: Roots are another popular choice for essential oil production as they contain a higher amount of zingiberene, a compound that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Buds: Buds are often used for their high content of sesquiterpenes, including zingiberene and bisabolol, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and clean them thoroughly.
Steam distill the cleaned rhizomes at 100-150 psi for 2 hours to release oils.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water and other impurities.
Filter the oil through a charcoal filter to remove any remaining contaminants and impurities.
Bottle the filtered oil in dark glass containers and store it in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 fresh stalks of cymbopogon citratus in the morning when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Dry the harvested stalks for 7 days in a well-ventilated area exposed to direct sunlight.
Steam distill 20 kg of dried stalks using a 10:1 water to plant material ratio for 8 hours.
Separate the distilled oil from the water and filter it through a 100-micron filter for 30 minutes.
Store the resulting essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities immediately.
Store the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid and label it clearly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with neck pain because it contains compounds like cineole and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When applied topically, these compounds can reduce swelling and ease muscle tension in the neck, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort. The oil's antimicrobial properties can also help prevent infection and promote healing in the affected area, further alleviating pain and promoting overall well-being. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to any pain management routine.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This monoterpenoid helps with neck pain by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic, reducing pain and inflammation in the affected area.
Linalool: This monoterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate muscle spasms and reduce pain in the neck region.
Terpinen-4-ol: This monoterpenoid has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that may contribute to neck pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils for neck pain due to their high concentration of compounds with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Buds: The buds are used to make essential oils for neck pain due to their high concentration of compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make essential oils for neck pain due to their high concentration of compounds with antispasmodic properties that can help relieve muscle tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 kg of fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia at the right stage of maturity.
Clean the leaves thoroughly to remove any dirt or contaminants.
Steam distill the leaves in a still for 3-4 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Collect the essential oil from the distillate using a separator or decanter.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the affected area. The oil's analgesic properties also help to numb the pain, providing quick relief from tension and strain. Additionally, the oil's antispasmodic properties can help to relax the muscles, reducing muscle spasms and cramps that often contribute to neck pain. This combination of properties makes Salvia officinalis essential oils a valuable natural remedy for neck pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Tricin: Helps reduce inflammation associated with neck pain by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent.
Salvianolic acid A: Works as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce pain and discomfort caused by neck strain.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing pain and inflammation in the neck area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in compounds such as rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe neck pain.
Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis contain camphor and borneol, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can provide relief from neck pain.
Flowers: The flowers of Salvia officinalis are a good source of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh or dried leaves of salvia officinalis in the morning when the oil content is highest approximately 3 ounces.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area at a temperature not exceeding 104 degrees fahrenheit for 2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a distillation apparatus with a water-to-herb ratio of 10 to 1.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel and discard the water portion.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove impurities and debris.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum essential oils helps with neck pain because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to reduce muscle spasms and relax tense muscles, alleviating discomfort and stiffness in the neck area. Additionally, the oil's warming and soothing effects can help increase blood circulation, promoting healing and reducing pain. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from neck pain, promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This constituent has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation in the neck region.
Linalool: It has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to the relief of pain and discomfort in the neck area.
Eugenol: As an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, eugenol helps alleviate pain and reduce swelling associated with neck strain.

Barks: The bark is a primary source of cinnamon oil, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relieve neck pain.
